name: Credit.org
description_short: Nonprofit HUD-approved financial counseling agency founded in 1974. Offers free and low-cost counseling for housing, debt, credit, and foreclosure prevention.
description_long:
Credit.org is a nonprofit financial counseling organization founded in 1974 and headquartered in Riverside, California. With over five decades of operation, it is one of the country's longer-established nonprofit counseling agencies. The organization holds HUD-approved housing counseling agency status, which makes it an authorized provider of the mandatory HECM (Home Equity Conversion Mortgage) counseling required by the federal government before any reverse mortgage can close. Certified counselors work one-on-one with clients rather than using automated systems, and services are available in both English and Spanish.

Credit.org's services span three primary domains. In housing, they provide reverse mortgage counseling (both HECM and non-HECM), pre-purchase homebuyer counseling, foreclosure prevention counseling, reverse mortgage default assistance, and disaster recovery financial counseling. On the debt side, they offer debt relief counseling, debt management plans (DMPs), and bankruptcy options counseling. Credit services include credit card debt counseling, credit score improvement guidance, and credit report review. They also offer rental readiness and affordability counseling, plus FIT Academy — a self-paced online education platform with courses on budgeting, debt, mortgage, and foreclosure prevention. Employer financial wellness programs are an additional offering for corporate clients.

What sets Credit.org apart is the scale of its track record: 5.2 million counseling sessions completed, $2.2 billion in homeowner support delivered, and 87,000+ participants through their online education programs. As a nonprofit, counselors have no financial incentive to upsell products or steer clients toward particular lenders or debt settlement companies. The $99 fee for reverse mortgage counseling is a flat, regulated charge (required by HUD) rather than a commission or service markup — and fee waivers are available for those who cannot afford even that.

Credit.org is a strong fit for consumers who need unbiased expert guidance rather than a product sale. Their main limitation is that they are not a credit repair company: they do not dispute credit report items, send goodwill letters, or file complaints on a client's behalf. They are also not a lender — no funds change hands. Debt management plan fees are not publicly listed, which requires clients to inquire directly. Anyone seeking aggressive credit repair or fast-cash lending should look elsewhere; for everyone else facing financial complexity — especially around housing — Credit.org is among the most credentialed and affordable options available.

pricing.guarantee_details: Nonprofit model — no guarantee applicable. Fee waivers available for clients unable to afford service fees.

tiers.name: Reverse Mortgage Counseling
features: Fee waiver available for qualifying clients

tiers.name: FIT Academy (Online Education)
features: Self-paced online courses on debt, credit, mortgage, budgeting

pros: Nonprofit with no commission incentive — counselors cannot profit from product referrals
Founded in 1974 with 52 years of continuous operation
5.2 million+ counseling sessions completed across their history
HUD-approved for mandatory HECM reverse mortgage counseling — federally recognized credential
Reverse mortgage counseling fee is a flat $99 with waivers available for those who cannot pay
Handles housing AND debt counseling under one roof — rare combination for a single nonprofit
Bilingual service delivery in English and Spanish
cons: Does not dispute credit report items or engage in credit repair — different service entirely from credit repair firms
Debt management plan fees and standard counseling session fees are not publicly listed on the website
No mobile app and no real-time score tracking tool
Not a lender — cannot provide any direct funds, emergency cash, or loans
NFCC membership and other certifications beyond HUD-approval were not confirmed from available website content
best_for: Homeowners facing foreclosure who need free, unbiased counseling before engaging a servicer
Seniors age 62+ considering a reverse mortgage who must complete mandatory HUD counseling
People overwhelmed by credit card debt seeking a debt management plan through a nonprofit (not a settlement company)
First-time homebuyers who want pre-purchase financial counseling before applying for a mortgage
diagnosis: Credit.org is best for consumers who need certified, unbiased financial counseling — particularly around housing, reverse mortgages, or debt management — and want to avoid for-profit companies with conflicted incentives. With 52 years of operation and 5.2 million sessions completed, it is one of the most credentialed nonprofit counseling agencies in the country. The main caveat: this is not credit repair and not a lender — anyone seeking aggressive dispute filing or fast access to cash will need a different type of provider.
services: FIT Academy self-paced online financial education
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
